plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Trim QQQI and SPY to reduce exposure and concentration.
Add to CRWD to increase quality software exposure.
Initiate a position in XLE for energy sector diversification.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with reduced leverage.
Expect lower volatility and a modest return target.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target of around 1.5% per month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a larger cash buffer for safety. Drawdowns should be minimal.

plan: Trim SOXL and sell SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI and SPY to lower income and index concentration.
Buy NVDU to add a high-scoring tech setup.
Add to AMAT to increase semiconductor exposure.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction.
It aims for steady growth with controlled drawdowns.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% per month by reducing leverage and concentration while adding quality setups. It aims for risk-first growth, blending broad market exposure with specific tech opportunities. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Increase SOXL and consider adding SPAL for leveraged exposure.
Sell QQQI to remove income focus.
Trim SPY to reduce broad index concentration.
Buy ERX and NVDU to capitalize on strong scanner setups.
This plan aggressively pursues growth using leverage.
Expect higher potential returns but also significantly larger drawdowns and leverage decay.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month by increasing leverage and concentrating in high-scoring setups, including leveraged ETFs. It accepts higher potential upside but also significantly larger drawdowns and the effects of leverage decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta CRWD.
Significantly increase QQQI for income generation.
Trim SPY to maintain broad index exposure.
Add XLE to diversify income sources.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions.
Expect low volatility and reliable income, not high growth.
income.read: This income-focused plan targets around 1% per month by rotating into income-generating assets and cutting high-risk positions. It prioritizes capital preservation and reliable distributions over growth. Drawdowns are expected to be minimal, but NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
